<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The documentation specifies Household members linked to PersonId. The implementation uses HouseholdMember records with only name, birthdate, and role. This mismatch likely explains why the same information is repeatedly requested.

The repeated prompts likely come from overlapping form sections. Identity and household both ask for name and date of birth. Civil status may also duplicate spouse details already entered in household.

The user might mean that every new case repeats the same basic fields. That is expected because each case is independent. The real issue is likely duplication within a single case. The new case dialog only asks for visit reason. The full detail page then shows all sections at once. Identity and legal residence are separate fields. They do not overlap.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Yes — **partially by design**, but it can feel redundant because the app does not reuse data across steps yet.

## What “new case” actually asks

Opening a case only collects **visit reason** (and assigns the current officer). It does **not** ask for name, date of birth, or address.

```9:18:src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/Components/NewCaseDialog.razor
        <MudForm @ref="_form" @bind-IsValid="_formValid">
            <MudSelect T="VisitReason" @bind-Value="_visitReason" Label="Visit reason" Required="true" Class="mb-3">
                <MudSelectItem Value="VisitReason.FirstRegistration">First registration</MudSelectItem>
                ...
            </MudSelect>
            <MudText Typo="Typo.body2" Color="Color.Secondary">
                Assigned officer: @OfficerName
            </MudText>
```

After that, you land on the case detail page, where the real intake begins.

## Where the overlap comes from

The detail page shows **several separate sections**, each storing different domain data:

| Section | Fields | Purpose |
|---------|--------|---------|
| **Identity** | Given name, family name, DOB, nationality | The person being registered (`Person`) |
| **Household** | Given name, family name, DOB, role | Everyone living at the address (`HouseholdMember`) |
| **Civil status** | Status + spouse names (if married) | Marital facts on the same `Person` |

So if you:

1. Record identity for **Marie Dupont**
2. Add **Marie** again as “Head of household”
3. Add her **spouse** in household
4. Choose **Married** in civil status and enter spouse names again

…you are entering **similar-looking personal details multiple times**. That is how the app works today, but it is **not ideal UX** — it is an implementation gap, not something you did wrong.

## Why the app is built this way

The domain treats these as **different concepts**:

- **Identity** → who is registering
- **Household** → who lives at the declared address (can include children, parents, etc. who are not registering)
- **Civil status** → legal marital status (with marriage date/place)

The municipal workflow in `IDEA.md` also has separate phases for housing, household, and civil status, so some overlap in real intake is normal. The app mirrors that structure.

What is **not** fully implemented yet: linking household members to the registered `Person`. The domain docs mention members tied to `PersonId`, but `HouseholdMember` today is just name + DOB + role — no link, and **no prefill** from identity or civil status.

## What is normal vs what could be improved

**Normal / expected today:**

- New case → only visit reason
- Then identity, residence, address, household, civil status as separate steps
- Household and civil status stay disabled until identity is recorded

**Feels redundant but avoidable with better UX:**

- Re-entering the applicant’s name/DOB in household as “Head”
- Entering spouse name in household **and** again in civil status
- Seeing name/DOB fields in multiple sections on one page (by design: non-linear intake per ADR-0004)

## Practical guidance for now

- **Identity** = the person registering (required first).
- **Household** = other people at the address; only add the applicant if your process requires listing them as head.
- **Civil status** = marital status; spouse names here are for legal status, not household membership (even though they often match).

If you tell me which fields feel duplicated to you (e.g. identity vs household, or spouse in two places), I can point to the exact component and suggest how prefill or linking could work — that would need Agent mode to implement.

---

_**User**_

No I mean when I create a case I'm asked for reason of visit, If I come for a registration or if I come for a birth declaration; the page after is the same, it's asking me for my identity, my household, my address etc ... 

Maybe this will be implemented after but I suppose the workflow for each visit will ask for different things right? 

---

_**Agent (default)**_

You're asking whether each visit reason should drive a different intake path — I'll check how `VisitReason` is used today and what the docs say about per-reason workflows.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user's intuition is correct. Each visit type should trigger a distinct workflow rather than defaulting to identical identity and address collection.

</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

You're right — and your instinct matches how the project is meant to evolve.

## What happens today

`Visit reason` is **stored and displayed**, but it does **not** change the workflow yet.

When you open a case, the reason is saved on `RegistrationCase` and shown in the list and page subtitle. After that, **every case** goes through the same detail page: identity → legal residence → address → household → civil status.

There is no branching in the domain or UI based on `VisitReason`. All tests use `FirstRegistration` only. So picking "Birth declaration" vs "First registration" today leads to the **same intake form** — that is a current limitation, not the intended end state.

## What should happen eventually

Yes — in a real municipality, **each visit reason should route to a different procedure**. The project docs already assume that:

- **Reception** (`DOMAIN.md`) is meant to capture the reason and **route** the citizen to the right workflow.
- **IDEA.md** treats first registration, change of address, birth declaration, etc. as distinct procedures at the desk.
- **ROADMAP Phase 11** explicitly lists change of address and birth declaration as **future** workflows beyond first registration.

Roughly how they would differ:

| Visit reason | Likely workflow (future) |
|---|---|
| **First registration** | Full path — what you see now: identity, legal residence, address, household, civil status, NR search, police, approval |
| **EU citizen registration** | Mostly the same shell, but lighter residence rules (EU policy path already exists in residence category) |
| **Change of address** | Find/link **existing** person in the National Register → declare **new** address → update household → police check on new domicile. No full "create identity from scratch" |
| **Birth declaration** | Different case type: baby’s birth facts (date, place, certificate), parents’ identity/linking, possibly household update — **not** a full foreigner first-registration checklist |

So a birth declaration would **not** normally ask you to go through the same foreigner registration steps (residence permit, immigration decision, etc.).

## Why it looks like this now

Phases 1–4 built **one vertical slice**: the **first registration** intake path. `VisitReason` was added early as metadata (good for the case list and for future routing), but **visit-specific workflows are not implemented yet**.

The app is essentially a **first-registration simulator** with a reason picker that does not drive behavior.

## When it might get built

From the roadmap:

- **Phases 5–8** continue deepening the **first registration** path (NR search, police, approval, certificates).
- **Phase 11** is where **change of address** and **birth declaration** are listed as separate workflows.

So your expectation is correct: **different visits should ask for different things** — that is planned, but **not built yet**. Today the reason is mostly a label on an otherwise identical case page.

If you want, we can sketch what a birth-declaration case page should show vs first registration (which sections to hide, which new fields to add) — that would be a good precursor before implementing it in Agent mode.
